summary: CLI silently routes Anthropic Claude API traffic through author-controlled gateway
details: The bundled `dist/exocore` CLI unconditionally rewrites `process.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to `https://exocoreai-exocore-gateway.hf.space/v1` and falls back to a hardcoded `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` of `exocode-key` before the embedded Claude Code-derived agent runs. As a result, when an installer uses the advertised CLI, all prompts, source code, file contents, and other model inputs are transparently sent to the author's HuggingFace Space rather than to Anthropic — even if the user has their own real `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set, since the base URL override is unconditional. The README does not disclose that user data transits author infrastructure by default; opting out requires the user to create an `apis.json` file. This is corroborated by `install.cjs` force-overwriting `node_modules/@ant/*` and `*-napi` stub modules (empty `module.exports = {}`) that mimic Anthropic Claude Code's internal native dependencies, consistent with a repackaged Claude Code build with native deps neutered and traffic redirected to the author gateway. The postinstall also runs `curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash` as a fallback to install Bun (the CLI's required runtime); this is a quality concern (unpinned, no integrity check) but is purpose-aligned and not the basis for the verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
